description: Cards are content containers used to present a group of information. Every card must use the standard card-header / card-body structure. card-footer is optional and reserved for action buttons.

always: Always include a `card-header` and `card-body` inside every `.card` — these provide responsive padding and alignment.
Use `card-footer` only when action buttons are appropriate — it is optional.
Use `bg-transparent` on `card-header` and `card-footer` when you do not want the default background color.
Place `card-title` inside `card-header` — it receives bold 16px styling automatically.
never: Do not use `card-secondary-navigation` and `card-primary-navigation` together on the same card.
Do not use `card-search` for cards that do not need a fixed-height scrollable body.

Basic.description: A simple information card with a header and body. Use for read-only data display where no actions are needed.

examples.Information Card: Patient Details

Search.description: Card used for search filter forms. Header holds the card title, body holds the form fields, footer holds Reset and Search action buttons.

elements.footer: card-footer bottom-buttons-footer d-flex justify-content-end bg-transparent pt-0

rules: Always place Reset and Search buttons in the card-footer, not the card-body.
Reset button uses `btn btn-secondary-text`, Search button uses `btn btn-primary`.
Search.template:
{title}

{formContent}

examples.Search Card: Search Filters
